summary: copy_to_audio_dir doesn't ensure audio_dir exists before copy
failure_scenario: Fresh install where audio_dir was never created, or user deleted the audio directory. std::fs::copy will fail with 'No such file or directory' (ENOENT).

summary: dest.to_str().ok_or('Invalid UTF-8')? loses path on non-UTF-8 Windows/Linux paths
failure_scenario: On Windows with non-UTF-8 paths (rare but possible via legacy codepages), or Linux with non-UTF-8 filenames. PathBuf::to_str() returns None for non-UTF-8 paths, causing empty audio_path in DB -> broken playback.

summary: DB error handler ignores remove_file failure (let _ = std::fs::remove_file(&copied_path))
failure_scenario: DB insert fails (e.g., constraint violation, disk full), AND remove_file fails (file locked by AV, permission denied, filesystem error). Orphan file remains on disk with no DB row.

summary: validate_source only checks exists(), not is_file()
failure_scenario: User passes a directory path (e.g., /home/user/Music). exists() returns true, is_file() would be false. copy_to_audio_dir calls std::fs::copy which fails with 'Is a directory' error.

summary: invoke<{ id: string }> type too narrow, Rust returns full Meeting
failure_scenario: TypeScript type says only { id: string }, but Rust returns full Meeting struct (id, title, audio_path, duration_secs, created_at, updated_at, status). TS will error if code accesses meeting.title, meeting.audio_path, etc. Line 49 uses meeting.id which works, but any future access breaks.

summary: Error format changed: 'Unsupported file format: .{ext}' vs old '{ext}'
failure_scenario: Frontend error matching (if any) expects old format. Also test line 112 checks err.starts_with('Unsupported file format') which still passes. But if frontend had exact string matching, it would break.

summary: DB lock error message includes 'DB lock poisoned' but original error lost (e.to_string())
failure_scenario: Mutex poisoned (thread panicked while holding lock). Error shows 'DB lock poisoned: PoisonError { ... }' but actual panic cause is lost. Hard to debug root cause.

summary: Mock returns duration_secs in create_meeting but not in import_meeting
failure_scenario: Test calls import_meeting and expects duration_secs (e.g., groupByDate function in meeting-list.tsx line 205 expects duration_secs). Mock returns undefined -> groupByDate crashes or sorts wrong.

summary: No // ponytail: comment marking atomic import+rollback as deliberate simplification
failure_scenario: Code comment at lines 36-38 describes the atomic behavior but doesn't use the // ponytail: convention per project CLAUDE.md. Maintainers may not recognize this as a deliberate tradeoff with upgrade path.

summary: Two 'Import Audio' buttons (header + empty state) duplicate component logic
failure_scenario: Header button (line ~70) and empty state button (line ~120) both call handleImportAudio. Not a bug but duplication. Any change to import UX needs updating both.
Wait - line 44 actually uses filePath.split(/[\\/]/).pop() which handles both. This candidate is FACTUALLY WRONG - the code already handles Windows paths.

summary: async fn import_meeting doesn't .await anything, could be sync fn
failure_scenario: Function is marked async but does no await. Tauri commands can be sync or async; making it async adds unnecessary overhead (Future allocation). No functional bug, just unnecessary async.

summary: validate_source does exists() check, then copy_to_audio_dir does std::fs::copy which also checks existence. Double stat syscall
failure_scenario: validate_source calls source.exists() (stat), then copy_to_audio_dir calls std::fs::copy which does its own stat. Two syscalls for same check. Negligible perf impact but real duplication.

summary: ALLOWED_EXTS constant not shared with frontend (could diverge)
failure_scenario: Frontend meeting-list.tsx line 35 has its own array: ['wav', 'mp3', 'm4a', 'aac', 'ogg', 'flac', 'webm']. If backend adds 'opus' but frontend doesn't, user can select file but import fails. Or vice versa.
